metadata.title: Ripple/XRP and the TradFi rails — the documented core, the SEC outcome, Hidden Road/Ripple Prime, and the ISO 20022 / SWIFT / JPM / BIS / IMF / World Bank claims (documented vs rumored, dated)
metadata.status: the SEC outcome, the Hidden Road deal, the SWIFT ISO 20022 cutover, JPM Coin/Kinexys, and the BIS/IMF/World Bank standards roles are FACT (court records, SEC, SWIFT, company + institution disclosures); the popular 'ISO 20022 certifies XRP' / 'banks/IMF/BIS run on XRP' claims are GRADED and largely REFUTED or UNVERIFIED — not adopted; extends existing Ripple/XRP/SEC/JPM nodes
metadata.frame: Sub-block of the Ripple/XRPL investigation (#171). Separates the DOCUMENTED Ripple↔traditional-finance facts from the RUMORED ones the community treats as confirmed. Documented: the SEC case ended (2025) with Torres's 2023 split ruling final ($125M penalty); Ripple bought prime broker Hidden Road ($1.25B) to launch Ripple Prime; SWIFT completed its ISO 20022 cutover (22 Nov 2025); JPMorgan built its OWN deposit token (JPM Coin → Kinexys), not XRP. Rumored/refuted: 'ISO 20022 certifies XRP', 'SWIFT/JPM/IMF/BIS settle on XRP'. The discipline (per #171): ISO 20022 is a MESSAGING STANDARD that endorses no asset; absence of a public XRP integration is not proof of a hidden one. Extends Ripple, XRP, XRPL, RLUSD, Ripple_Prime, SEC, JPMorgan, JPM_Coin, Brad_Garlinghouse, Stuart_Alderoty, David_Schwartz.

the_documented_core: Ripple Labs (fka OpenCoin, 2012), co-founded by Chris Larsen + Jed McCaleb + Arthur Britto, with David Schwartz (CTO), Brad Garlinghouse (CEO), Stuart Alderoty (CLO/President of legal); it controls a large share of the 100B XRP premine via on-ledger escrow. Products: XRP Ledger (XRPL), Ripple Payments / ODL (On-Demand Liquidity, formerly xRapid), and RLUSD (a USD stablecoin, NYDFS-approved Dec 2024). grade: fact.
the_sec_outcome: SEC v. Ripple (filed 22 Dec 2020): Judge Torres's 13 Jul 2023 summary judgment held Ripple's INSTITUTIONAL XRP sales were unregistered securities offerings, but PROGRAMMATIC/exchange sales and distributions were NOT — a partial, much-cited split. Aug-2024 remedies: a $125M civil penalty + injunction. Both sides appealed; a proposed settlement to cut the penalty (~$50M) and lift the injunction was BLOCKED by Judge Torres (2025); the parties then DROPPED their appeals (Aug 2025), leaving the 2023 ruling and $125M penalty as the final word. grade: fact (with the conflicting $50M-settlement attempt noted, dated).
the_prime_brokerage: Ripple's institutional push: it acquired prime broker Hidden Road for $1.25B (announced 8 Apr 2025; closed Oct 2025) — becoming the first crypto firm to run a global, multi-asset PRIME BROKERAGE (rebranded Ripple Prime; Hidden Road also won a FINRA broker-dealer license). Part of an acquisition streak (also Rail, GTreasury, etc.). grade: fact.
the_iso20022_claim: The central ARG-adjacent claim, GRADED: SWIFT completed its migration of FI-to-FI cross-border messaging to ISO 20022 (MT→ISO coexistence ended 22 Nov 2025). ISO 20022 is a DATA/MESSAGING STANDARD — it defines how payment messages are structured; it does NOT 'certify', list, or endorse any cryptocurrency. The community 'ISO 20022 coin list' (XRP/XLM/HBAR/QNT/XDC/ADA/ALGO) is a marketing/community construct, not an ISO or SWIFT designation. Ripple markets XRP as a bridge asset within RippleNet's ISO-20022-ready stack, and is 'technically aligned' — but that is alignment, not adoption-by-banks. grade: 'ISO 20022 = banks will use XRP' is WEAK/UNSUPPORTED; the standard cutover is fact.
the_jpm_and_institutions: What the big institutions actually built/do (refuting 'they run on XRP'): JPMorgan built its OWN bank deposit token + blockchain unit — JPM Coin, now Kinexys — NOT XRP-based. The BIS (and its CPMI) PROMOTES ISO 20022 for cross-border payments and runs CBDC/tokenization pilots (mBridge, Agorá) on bespoke DLT, NOT XRPL. The IMF and World Bank promote ISO 20022 in payment-system modernization/development; the 'IMF SDR will be XRP' claim is UNVERIFIED — the SDR is a fiat-currency basket, not a crypto asset. Ripple has PARTICIPATED in WEF digital-asset/CBDC working groups (participation, not institutional adoption). grade: institutional facts documented; the XRP-adoption versions unverified/refuted.
the_honest_reading: The documented Ripple↔TradFi picture is substantial but narrower than the lore: a real (partial) SEC win, a real prime-brokerage build-out (Hidden Road/Ripple Prime), a real stablecoin (RLUSD), and genuine technical alignment with the ISO 20022 standard that global finance is adopting. What is NOT documented — and is widely asserted as if it were — is that SWIFT, JPMorgan, the BIS, the IMF, or the World Bank SETTLE on XRP or that ISO 20022 'certifies' it. The standard cutover (22 Nov 2025) is fact; the leap to 'banks now use XRP' is the red herring. Per #171's discipline, the absence of a public XRP integration at these institutions is treated as absence of evidence, not proof of a hidden one. Both documented and rumored kept separate, dated. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: Bloomberg Law — Ripple, SEC drop appeal after settlement path blocked (2025)
Ripple — closes $1.25B Hidden Road acquisition, launches Ripple Prime (Oct 2025)
SWIFT — ISO 20022 for financial institutions (the standard; Nov-2025 cutover)
BIS — Project mBridge / Agorá (cross-border CBDC/tokenization on bespoke DLT)
JPMorgan — Kinexys (fka Onyx; JPM Coin deposit token)

edges.raw_instrument: acquired prime broker Hidden Road for $1.25B (announced 8 Apr 2025; closed Oct 2025)
rebranded as Ripple Prime — first crypto-firm global multi-asset prime broker; FINRA broker-dealer license
positions XRP/ODL (On-Demand Liquidity) as a cross-border ALTERNATIVE to SWIFT correspondent banking — competition, not adoption
migrated FI-to-FI cross-border messaging to ISO 20022; MT->ISO coexistence ended 22 Nov 2025 — a data STANDARD, endorses no asset

RippleNet/XRP 'technically aligned' with ISO 20022 — BUT ISO 20022 certifies NO cryptocurrency; the 'ISO 20022 coin list' (XRP/XLM/HBAR/QNT/XDC/ADA/ALGO) is community/marketing, not an ISO/SWIFT endorsement
built its OWN bank deposit token + blockchain unit (JPM Coin -> Kinexys) — NOT XRP-based (refutes 'JPM runs on XRP')
BIS/CPMI promotes ISO 20022 for cross-border payments; its CBDC/tokenization pilots (mBridge, Agorá) use bespoke DLT, NOT XRPL
promotes ISO 20022 in payment-system modernization; the 'IMF SDR = XRP bridge asset' claim is UNVERIFIED (the SDR is a fiat-currency basket, not crypto)
promotes ISO 20022 for payment-system development; no documented XRP integration
participated in WEF digital-asset/CBDC working groups — participation, NOT institutional adoption of XRP
